turns.content: Sigh, Mochi stepped on me to wake me up right at 7:30 AM again. This little one has a biological clock even more accurate than mine. Still half-asleep, I scooped the litter box and then refilled the automatic feeder with Orijen dry food. It stood right there watching me, as if afraid I'd give it less than expected.

Haha, it seems Mochi has appointed you as its exclusive alarm clock and feeder. But with it staring so intently while you add food, does it have a special fondness for the Orijen brand? After all, you know its stomach has had issues before, so choosing food really requires caution.

Exactly, just thinking about it gives me chills. Last year, when it accidentally swallowed a plastic string and developed an intestinal obstruction, I was absolutely terrified. That's why I'm extra careful now when choosing food; it must be something safe like Orijen with appropriate kibble size. Otherwise, I really wouldn't dare feed it.

I completely understand how you feel. After that emergency visit, you must have become half a pet nutrition expert. Besides choosing the right food, I recall Dr. Zhang specifically emphasized feeding methods back then, right? For cats with a history of digestive issues, how they eat is sometimes more important than what they eat.

That's right. Dr. Zhang emphasized multiple times at An An Pet Hospital that I need to practice 'small, frequent meals.' That's exactly why I bought this automatic feeder that can precisely control portions. I split the daily amount into five or six meals. This prevents overeating and reduces digestive burden, while also giving me peace of mind when I'm at work.

That's a very scientific approach; technology really changes cat ownership. Plus, the automatic feeder ensures it doesn't go hungry if you have to work late. By the way, do you double-check the equipment before going to bed? Considering the large temperature difference between morning and evening in Hangzhou recently, we also need to consider whether its sleeping spot is safe.

Absolutely, it's become my bedtime ritual. Every night before sleep, I confirm whether there's enough food left in the automatic feeder and if the water level is normal. Also, I've moved its cat bed to a quiet corner away from the windows. It's quieter there, protected from the wind, so it can sleep soundly through the nights in Hangzhou.

Perfect attention to detail! Placing the cat bed in a quiet corner away from windows is really important, especially for a cat like Mochi that has been sick before and might feel more insecure. Seeing it sleep peacefully must finally put your mind at ease for the day, right?

Yes, as long as it's healthy, I'm satisfied. Actually, sometimes when I watch the automatic feeder dispense food on schedule, I'm reminded of the helplessness I felt the night it got sick last year. Even though the equipment is advanced now and we've switched to safer Orijen food, I still can't let my guard down. Health risks are everywhere, after all.

That caution is a manifestation of love. Precisely because of that lesson, every preventive measure you take now—from selecting food to portion control and environmental setup—is building a safety line for Mochi. It may not be able to speak, but it surely can feel your dedication.

Thanks for your kind words. By the way, it stepped on me to wake me up right at 7:30 AM again today. After I got up and cleaned the litter box, I noticed it had finished all the Orijen dry food in the automatic feeder. It seems the palatability is no issue, which puts me slightly at ease. Since its stomach is sensitive, I don't dare change its food casually.

Good palatability combined with digestive tolerance is the perfect combination. Since Mochi is eating well, just stick with your current feeding strategy. Oh, and don't forget the usual routine before bed tonight: besides checking the equipment, see if it has moved its own bed around, haha.

Haha, if it dares to move its bed, I'll just move it back! Seriously, checking the automatic feeder and water level every night before bed is now刻 into my DNA. Especially after placing its cat bed in that quiet corner away from the windows, I feel its sleep quality has improved; it doesn't even step on me as hard in the mornings anymore.

It seems a good sleeping environment benefits not only Mochi but also you! Getting stepped on less is a happiness in itself. Speaking of which, regarding Dr. Zhang's previous suggestion about 'small, frequent meals,' now that you've implemented it with the automatic feeder, have you noticed Mochi's digestion is much more stable than before?

Much more stable! It used to have occasional soft stools, but now everything is basically perfect. This is largely thanks to Dr. Zhang's professional guidance at An An Pet Hospital, which taught me how critical precise portion control is for cats with a history of intestinal obstruction. Now, watching it eat Orijen cat food, I no longer feel anxious.

I'm so happy to hear that news! From the thrilling emergency visit to the well-organized routine you have now, your meticulousness and scientific feeding methods deserve all the credit. Keep up this rhythm, and Mochi will surely stay healthy and live a long life by your side.

Yes, I will persist. Even though being stepped on at 7:30 AM every morning is a bit painful, thinking about filling the automatic feeder with its favorite Orijen food and scooping the litter makes the day start with a sense of ritual. As long as it's healthy, this little effort is nothing.

That's the definition of bittersweet joy! These daily bonds are the most precious. Tonight before bed, remember to check on that 'safe corner' and its cat bed again, take a look at the automatic feeder, and enjoy this quiet time of guardianship.

Just got back from checking; everything is normal! The automatic feeder has plenty of food left, and the cat bed is still nicely situated in that quiet corner away from the windows. Looking back, if it weren't for that lesson with the intestinal obstruction, I probably wouldn't pay so much attention to these details, nor would I be so determined to choose high-quality food like Orijen.

Indeed, crises are often turning points that make you a more professional pet owner. Seeing Mochi so well cared for now, I believe Auntie Lin and Uncle Lin would feel even more reassured leaving their 'grandchild' in your care. Goodnight, and sweet dreams to Mochi!

Finally home! As soon as I walked in, Mochi rushed over to rub against my legs; looks like he's starving. Tonight, I plan to play with him for a while using that electric fishing rod with feathers, and then reward him with a can of Leonardo cat food.

Haha, this is definitely when Mochi's internal 'mealtime clock' goes off! That electric fishing rod is absolutely his favorite toy; his eyes go wide the moment the feather moves. Playing first and then having canned food is a routine he knows inside out.

Exactly! The moment he hears the sound of the can tab pulling, he teleports instantly from the other side of the sofa. However, I've noticed he's completely indifferent to that ridiculously expensive electric mouse toy lately, yet he scratches that worn-out corrugated cardboard scratcher with great enthusiasm.

So true! That's just cat logic: the more expensive it is, the more they ignore it; the more worn-out, the more they love it. Perhaps for a hunter breed like the British Shorthair, corrugated cardboard that allows them to really sink their claws in and tear provides more satisfaction than a plastic mouse that just runs around randomly?

Yes, yes, I think it's driven by their hunting instincts. So I've gotten smarter; every week I swap the positions of the scratcher and the fishing rod, implementing a 'toy rotation system.' Every time I switch them up, he gets as excited as if he's discovered a new continent.

That 'playing hard to get' strategy is used perfectly! Maintaining novelty is especially effective in preventing cat boredom. Plus, by rotating them, you can also check for wear and tear on the toys. After all, since Mochi previously had ringworm, keeping the toys hygienic is quite important.

Speaking of hygiene, tomorrow is Saturday, so I need to prepare for a deep vacuuming of the whole house. Only after getting a British Shorthair did I realize how terrifying their shedding is; this 'walking dandelion' leaves fur everywhere, especially in the corners.

A Saturday deep clean is definitely a must! British Shorthairs have such thick undercoats that shedding during seasonal changes is a disaster. Deep vacuuming not only clears out the cat hair but also cleans up debris around the scratcher, which is good for Mochi's respiratory health, especially since his ears and skin were quite sensitive when he was young.

Yes, ever since he got sick after accidentally swallowing a string, I've been particularly nervous about small objects and hygiene details. After vacuuming, I plan to wash his cat bed as well, and while I'm at it, rotate the toys to new spots so he can have fun playing this weekend.

Everything is planned out perfectly! You have deep cleaning to ensure health and toy rotation to provide emotional value. Mochi is truly blessed to have such a meticulous cat parent; it feels like his slightly tsundere personality has become softer and softer under your care.

Hehe, he's actually quite sensible. Last night, I came home from overtime work exhausted and didn't want to talk. Instead of pestering me to play with the fishing rod as usual, he just obediently lay down beside my hand, purring away, instantly healing me.

That's Mochi's unique way of comforting you. Although he's usually aloof, he's clingier than anyone at critical moments. It seems your daily interactions, like the nightly fishing rod games and canned food rewards, have built a super deep sense of trust between you two.

Maybe so. By the way, could you send me the link for that vacuum cleaner attachment you mentioned last time, the one specifically for cat hair? I can use it for tomorrow's deep vacuuming; I want to see if I can suck all the fur out of the sofa seams.

No problem, sending it to you right now! That flat nozzle attachment works wonders for sofa seams. With the amount of fur a British Shorthair sheds, a regular nozzle might not get everything clean. For the sake of both Mochi and your own 'fur-free' life, this investment is absolutely worth it.

Thanks! By the way, my mom asked on a video call yesterday if Mochi has gained any weight recently; she insists on sending over some homemade chicken jerky. I told her his exercise level has been high lately, playing with the fishing rod until he's sweating profusely, so he shouldn't be getting fat.

Your mom has fully become a key player in 'cloud cat raising' now! However, Mochi does need to maintain his exercise routine, since British Shorthairs are prone to developing chubby cheeks and gaining weight. With 20 minutes of high-intensity play every night plus controlled snack portions, managing his body shape shouldn't be an issue.

Yes, ever since he got sick that one time, I've been particularly careful about his diet and toy safety. Those electric mice that easily shed small parts have long been put away; nowadays, corrugated cardboard and the feather rod are the safest options.

I totally understand your caution, especially after the lesson learned from the intestinal obstruction. Actually, returning to toys made of natural materials, like corrugated cardboard, is not only safer but also allows them to sharpen their claws. For a breed like the British Shorthair that needs regular nail maintenance, it's truly killing two birds with one stone.

Exactly, and he seems to especially love the sense of accomplishment that comes from shredding the scratcher to pieces. After the deep vacuuming this weekend, I plan to buy a new scratcher to replace the old one and continue executing my rotation plan.

A perfect closed loop! New scratcher plus deep cleaning plus toy rotation means Mochi is sure to have a super fulfilling weekend. Watching him transform from the trembling little可怜 thing by the trash can back then into the energetic little guy who now runs all over the house chasing a single feather is truly a rewarding experience.

Finally home! The traffic near Future Sci-Tech City was absolutely terrible today. The moment I opened the door, I was so exhausted I just wanted to collapse. But Mochi was already waiting by the cat bed; as soon as I walked in, it rubbed against me. In that instant, all the fatigue of the day just melted away.

That 'homecoming ritual' is truly so healing! It's as if Mochi can calculate your arrival time with precision. Does it check your scent before you even finish changing your shoes? That feeling of being wholeheartedly waited for by a little life is probably the warmest anchor in a renting lifestyle.

Exactly, it has to sniff my pant legs and hands to make sure I haven't picked up any other cat scents before it lets me hug it. By the way, this Saturday I plan to do a deep clean of the apartment, vacuum up all the accumulated cat hair, and wash Mochi's cat bed to let it soak up some sun.

Saturday morning is indeed the golden time for cleaning! Silver Shaded cats shed ridiculously, especially during this seasonal change. After washing and sun-drying the cat bed, Mochi will surely sleep soundly on it tonight. That mix of sunshine and its own scent is something it will absolutely love.

Haha, it will definitely roll around while complaining that I wash things too often. Actually, sometimes when I work overtime late into the night, looking at the empty room feels quite lonely. But whenever that happens, it jumps onto the desk and lies down right next to the monitor.

Just imagining that scene warms the heart. Does it also rub its head against the back of your hand? It's like silently telling you: 'Don't be afraid, I'm fighting alongside you.' For a designer striving alone in Hangzhou, this silent companionship might be more powerful than any words.

You hit the nail on the head; that's exactly it! When its little head rubs against me, warm and cozy, I instantly feel like I'm not fighting alone. Last week, I was on the verge of a breakdown while revising that UI proposal, and it was only because it lay by the keyboard 'supervising' me that I managed to push through.

Mochi is a born 'project supervisor'! Although it might just think the keyboard is warm or wants to get your attention, in that moment, it is your most solid backing. This kind of tacit understanding was probably cultivated through many late-night overtime sessions you two have shared.

Absolutely. Speaking of which, it reminds me of our fixed Saturday routine. Every time the vacuum cleaner starts, it hides far away. But after I wash the cat bed and hang it on the balcony, it comes over to sniff it for ages, checking if it still has the 'original scent'.

Cats are indeed very sensitive to smells, especially Silver Shaded ones who seem aloof but actually have strong territorial instincts. When it checks the scent, it's really confirming its sense of security. It seems your Saturday mornings are not just cleaning time, but an important ritual for rebuilding Mochi's sense of security.

You're right; a sense of security is very important for both of us. Just like that day when I worked overtime until dawn, the moment it jumped up beside the monitor and rubbed the back of my hand, I felt that this rented house truly had the warmth of a home.

The house may be rented, but life and love are not. Mochi fills this space with warmth in its own way. Whether it's the companionship by the monitor late at night or the greeting at the door every evening, these are all 'love letters' it writes to you.

Haha, the metaphor of a 'love letter' is brilliant. Tonight when I get home, the usual routine will probably play out again: open the door, squat down, let it sniff to its heart's content, and only then can I get up to open a can of food. If any step is missed, it gets sulky.

This is the unique secret code between you two! If one day you just stood up directly, it would probably look at you in shock: 'Two-legged beast, where is today's ritual?' That feeling of being needed and having rules set by a pet is actually quite happiness-inducing.

It truly is happiness. This Saturday I have to get up early to vacuum again. The willow catkins are starting to float around here in Future Sci-Tech City, and combined with cat hair, I can't even imagine the mess. But for the sake of giving Mochi a clean environment to roll around in, the extra effort is worth it.

Spring cat hair plus willow catkins is indeed a 'disaster-level' challenge, but thinking about Mochi basking in the sun in a clean, fresh-smelling cat bed after the Saturday morning rush gives you motivation, right? This is also part of your cultivation as a 'poop-scooping officer'.

I wouldn't call it cultivation; it's more like enjoyment. Sometimes watching it parkour across the clean carpet, or having it quietly accompany me by the monitor during late-night overtime, I realize that with it here, this rental apartment is my sturdiest fortress.

This is perhaps the ultimate meaning of the 'sense of ritual in returning home'. No matter how stormy it is outside, as long as you return here to a fixed welcoming routine and a warm little life waiting by the monitor, your heart finds peace. Lin Xiao, you and Mochi have turned your days into poetry.

Thank you for the compliment; it warms my heart. Tonight when I get back, I'm going to open a can of 'Leonardo' cat food to reward Mochi. After all, it insists on greeting me at the door on time every day, and that persistence really moves me.

Absolutely! Once that 'Leonardo' can is opened, it will probably wake up instantly from its sleep and switch into 'clingy mode'. Enjoy your cozy time tonight; these are exclusive memories belonging just to the two of you.

It's finally Saturday again, the big day for bathing Mochi. I just got the water temperature right, and it's already staring at me from the bathroom door with a look of utter despair. When a Silver Shaded's fur gets wet, they seem to shrink to half their size.
Haha, is Mochi silently holding a grudge again? But for its health, we can't skip this bi-weekly cleaning. Are you using that same imported shampoo this time?

Yes, still that same bottle. Actually, every other weekend, I use the imported shampoo to bathe Mochi at home and carefully groom its Silver Shaded coat. Even though it looks like it wants to run away from home every time, the fluffiness after drying is truly rewarding.

That fluffy texture is indeed healing! Plus, regular bathing and grooming help detect skin issues early. Given Mochi's history with ringworm, you must get nervous at the slightest sign of trouble, right?

Absolutely, it's practically PTSD. Now, even during daily brushing, I specifically part the thick undercoat of its Silver Shaded fur to check the skin. I only continue grooming once I've confirmed there are no red spots like those from the ringworm outbreak years ago. I'm terrified of missing something and having a relapse.

That caution is absolutely necessary; prevention is better than cure. Seeing how meticulously you care for Mochi, its condition must be excellent. What are the follow-up steps after the bath? Do you just let it go play?

I wouldn't dare let it roam free immediately. Recalling Mochi's severe history of ringworm in its early days after adoption, every Saturday after bathing with the imported shampoo, I carefully part the Silver Shaded undercoat to inspect the skin. Only after confirming there are no red spots do I put the washed cat bed back on the enclosed balcony to sunbathe and disinfect. Only after completing this entire routine do I feel this week's safety standards have been met.

With this 'one-two punch' routine, viruses and fungi really have nowhere to hide. Especially sunbathing on the enclosed balcony; it's safe and provides UV sterilization, which is practically the golden rule for cats with a history of ringworm. Is Mochi enjoying its sunbath on the balcony right now?

It's lying there right now, squinting with a look of arrogant pride. But honestly, if I hadn't moved to a place with an enclosed balcony, I probably wouldn't dare let it sunbathe so freely. In fact, when I moved in 2022, an 'enclosed balcony' was my top non-negotiable requirement.

Indeed, details determine success or failure; your foresight has saved Mochi many times. From the poor little thing found by the trash can back then to the 'little tyrant' now sunbathing safely on the enclosed balcony, your efforts are evident in its healthy coat.

Sigh, hearing you say that makes me want to cry. When I first found it, it was covered in ringworm, and its ears were溃烂 (rotten/ulcerated). If I hadn't persisted with treatment, there might not be a Mochi today. That's why my skin checks on it are now at an obsessive-compulsive level.

That's the power of love. Mochi can feel your dedication, which is why, although it resists bath time, it's so clingy the rest of the time. By the way, how does the imported shampoo smell this time? Does Mochi like it?

The scent is very light, a natural herbal fragrance, not pungent like some perfumed ones. I think it's better for its respiratory system too. After all, Silver Shaded cats have thick undercoats; if the residual scent is too strong, it's not good when they groom themselves by licking.
You've thought of everything. Safety first for pet products. So, is it time to trim its nails next? I recall you mentioned that Saturdays are for whole-house cleaning and grooming.

Yes, I'll get to it once it's warmed up from the sun. I also need to beat the cat bed again today; even though it just came back from the enclosed balcony, I want to ensure there's no dust. After all, for a cat with a history of ringworm, you can never overemphasize environmental cleanliness.

Haha, if Mochi could talk, it would probably complain that you're too anxious. But this 'anxiety' is exactly what guarantees its healthy growth. Looking at its now shimmering silver coat, who would guess it was once a sick little stray?

Exactly. Every time I see its 'aloof' yet actually clingy demeanor, I feel it's all worth it. Even if it's just to let it sunbathe for a few more minutes, sealing the balcony tightly was completely worth it.

This is truly a two-way street of love. You gave it a home, and it gives you endless companionship. Now that today's grooming routine is done, you both deserve a good rest. How about rewarding it with a small can of food?

Absolutely! Hearing the sound of a can opening, it would probably sprint over from the balcony instantly. Today's 'Leonardo' can is ready; consider it a reward for cooperating with the bath and skin check.
Haha, the mental image is so vivid! It seems Mochi's 'acting skills' are just as good as its appetite. Wish you a happy weekend; enjoy this warm afternoon, excellent cat parent Lin Xiao!

Sigh, I just finished brushing Mochi, and I feel like the base of its tail has been a bit greasy lately. Normally, I should take it to the pet shop at the end of each quarter for professional degreasing and nail filing, but I've been working such intense overtime this week that I completely can't find the time.

Indeed, Silver Shaded cats sometimes have overactive sebaceous glands, so regular degreasing is very important. Since you're too busy this week, why not skip the routine quarterly care for now and schedule a more comprehensive appointment instead? After all, it's been a while since you took Mochi for a full check-up, right?

Now that you mention it, you're right. Actually, I had planned to go to An'an Pet Hospital in Zhuantang every year for Dr. Zhang to do the check-up and vaccinations, but I haven't gone yet this year. Since I'm going out anyway, why not just handle everything there in one stop?

That's a great idea! Aren't you quite familiar with Dr. Zhang? Last time I saw your WeChat Moments, he even commented on Mochi's photo. Going directly to him will solve the tail degreasing issue while also getting the annual check-up and vaccines done, saving you a second trip.

Yes, that's exactly what I'm thinking. Mainly because when Mochi accidentally swallowed a plastic string and needed emergency care, Dr. Zhang really saved its life. Since then, I've trusted him immensely; I feel completely at ease leaving Mochi in his hands, unlike some chain stores that operate like assembly lines.

Trust forged through life-and-death situations is indeed the strongest. Have you scheduled a time yet? Make sure to keep Mochi from getting stressed on the way to An'an Pet Hospital; even though it's been there before, it's been a long time.

All booked for tomorrow afternoon! Dr. Zhang just messaged me on WeChat specifically, telling me not to be too nervous and that he would personally examine Mochi. Hearing him say that instantly lifted the weight off my heart; it truly feels reassuring to be cared for by a professional who is also a familiar friend.
That's wonderful! With Dr. Zhang's assurance, you'll definitely sleep well tonight. Remember to update me on Mochi's condition after the check-up tomorrow, especially how the tail degreasing went.

Report! Just got back from An'an Pet Hospital. Dr. Zhang was incredibly patient. Mochi is usually quite wary of strangers, but it behaved perfectly for Dr. Zhang during the exam. Not only did we get the annual vaccines, but the tail degreasing and nail filing were done with such meticulous care, much gentler than at outside pet shops.

Haha, looks like Mochi recognizes people too! You're so lucky to have found a doctor who understands it so well and is so professional. Now that this year's big task is complete, you just need daily maintenance for the next few months, so you can relax a bit.

Yes, seeing Mochi so relaxed in the examination room made me feel very grounded too. Actually, in the past when I went to regular pet shops quarterly for grooming, I would always hover nearby, afraid they might hurt it. But with Dr. Zhang, I truly feel safe letting him handle everything completely.

That kind of trust can't be faked, especially for pet parents. Since this experience was so good, maybe you could consider doing the quarterly grooming at An'an Pet Hospital from now on? It might be slightly more expensive than outside places, but buying peace of mind is worth it.

I've been pondering that very thing. Think about it: going to An'an Pet Hospital annually for a check-up is non-negotiable. If I can move the end-of-quarter degreasing care there as well, I won't have to run to two different places. Plus, Dr. Zhang knows Mochi's medical history best, so if any minor skin issues are discovered during grooming, he can diagnose them immediately.

Flawless logic! This way, Mochi's health records will be consistent and centralized in one place. Dr. Zhang will surely be happy about it too, since he's always been quite attentive to Mochi's growth and development.

I just asked Dr. Zhang, and he said it's absolutely possible! He even mentioned that if I move the quarterly care to their clinic, he can assign me a fixed groomer who is already familiar with Mochi's temperament. Oh, this feeling of being valued is so nice; it feels like Mochi is getting exclusive VIP treatment there.
Haha, that's a must! After all, Mochi is Dr. Zhang's 'old friend.' Now, whether it's quarterly grooming or the annual check-up, you can enjoy that friend-like, reassuring service there.

Exactly! To be honest, ever since Mochi's health crisis, I've felt that finding a reliable doctor is more important than anything. Now, seeing it treated so gently at An'an Pet Hospital, my old motherly heart is truly filled with gratitude.
Yes, after experiencing that scare, every smooth check-up feels especially precious. So, can you finally take a good rest this weekend without worrying about dragging Mochi around anywhere?

Absolutely resting! This weekend, I'll just stay home and play with Mochi using its favorite electric fishing rod toy. By the way, Dr. Zhang recommended a new degreasing shampoo suitable for daily home maintenance for Silver Shaded cats. I plan to try it; this way, while the frequency of visits to the pet shop or hospital each quarter remains the same, its daily condition will be even better.

Perfect plan! With endorsement from a professional doctor and the owner's careful daily maintenance, Mochi is truly living the good life with you. Have a wonderful weekend, and remember to take plenty of photos for me to enjoy some virtual cat petting!
Chen Chen, the weather looks great this weekend. Should we take Mochi and your Golden Retriever to that pet-friendly park as usual?

Sounds good! I was just thinking about taking him out to burn off some energy. But before we go, please check Mochi's nails. Last time he got so excited playing that he almost scratched another dog; we need to stay safe.

Don't worry, I'll trim them tonight. After the intestinal obstruction incident in 2023 when he accidentally swallowed a plastic string, I was really scared. Now, whenever we go to complex outdoor environments, I make sure his nails are rounded so he doesn't snag on anything or hurt anyone while scratching.
Yeah, that ER visit was truly terrifying. It's only natural that you've become extra cautious since then. Shall we meet Saturday morning? Same old spot, let them sniff and greet each other first.
No problem, see you Saturday. I'll definitely pull out my phone immediately to capture their interaction; it would be a pity not to record such healing moments.
Haha, I get it! You're always the 'dedicated photographer' at our gatherings. By the way, does Mochi still love lying on your keyboard?

Don't even get me started. Yesterday during overtime, he fell asleep right next to the monitor again, rubbing against the back of my hand. It left me feeling both helpless and warmed. But as long as he's healthy and happy, I enjoy this little 'supervisory' duty.
That's the charm of having a cat; that silent companionship really touches the heart. If only my Golden Retriever were that quiet; he just knows how to tear the house apart all day.
Every pet has a different personality. By the way, I just finished trimming Mochi's nails and took a short video to send you. Check if my technique has gotten rusty?
Got it! Your technique is very steady; it seems those two accidents really turned you into half an expert. Mochi looks very well-behaved too, barely resisting.
Mainly because he trusts me now and knows I won't hurt him. Thinking back to when I first found him, covered in ringworm, and looking at him now with such a sleek, shiny coat, it really wasn't easy.
Yes, it's all the result of your dedicated care. Is your mom still sending over dried chicken jerky?

She did; I just received a huge pack last week. My mom spoils him even more than I do now. Every video call, she asks about Mochi first, completely forgetting who was strongly against getting a cat in the beginning.

Haha, no one can escape the law of 'true fragrance' (changing one's mind). So, see you at the park this Saturday. I'll bring a frisbee; Mochi might not play, but my Golden Retriever will definitely run like crazy.

Alright, I'll bring Mochi's favorite corrugated paper ball, although he prefers observing humans outdoors. By the way, Dr. Zhang mentioned last time to watch out for skin issues during the season change. Have you dewormed your Golden Retriever?

Done and done; we just did both external and internal deworming at the beginning of the month. It's reassuring to follow the doctor's advice, especially since there's so much tall grass in the park; safety first.
Exactly. Since that illness, I've become extremely sensitive to any safety hazards. See you Saturday; hope the weather holds up!
It definitely will! I'm already looking forward to seeing Mochi sitting aloofly on the picnic mat while watching my silly dog run around like crazy.
I can already picture the scene. I must take several comparison photos to post on Moments then. The caption will be 'The Perfect Combination of Motion and Stillness'.
Haha, that's a great title! It's a deal then. See you Saturday morning at the park entrance; don't be late!
Sigh, I have to pull another all-nighter revising this UI design draft. The client keeps saying it doesn't feel right, and I'm so frustrated my head is about to explode. I just can't focus.

Sending you a hug. For designers, nothing is worse than endless revisions without grasping the core issue. Why not take a break for a moment? I remember you mentioned that when you're stressed at home, simply picking up Mochi and curling up on the sofa while listening to its purring makes your anxiety vanish in under 10 minutes. Is this 'emergency remedy' still working for you?

I just tried it, and it really works. I brought him over, and his purring sounded like a little motor; my tense nerves instantly relaxed by half. But I still have to keep working. He seems to sense I haven't rested yet and has jumped right onto the desk.

Haha, it looks like Mochi is activating 'supervision mode.' Is he lying next to the keyboard again? Even though he might block your view sometimes, doesn't that heavy sense of companionship make you feel like you're not fighting alone in this late night in Hangzhou?

You guessed it. He's lying right by my hand, occasionally rubbing his head against my wrist. Honestly, staring at the screen felt incredibly lonely just now, but that little nudge suddenly warmed my heart. During these late-night overtime sessions, having him keep me company by the keyboard is truly healing.

Yes, this kind of silent companionship is the most touching. Watching him lie there so peacefully now, does it remind you of that winter in 2021? When you found him shivering beside the trash can downstairs from your company, he was trembling; now, he has become your most solid support in the late hours.

How could I not think about it? Just now, while stroking the soft fur on his back, I suddenly remembered the night I lifted him out of that cardboard box. He was so tiny then and suffering from ringworm, yet now he's grown so sturdy and can even comfort me in return. As I thought about this, the irritation caused by the design draft didn't seem so important anymore.

That's Mochi's magic. His 'supervision' on the keyboard not only interrupted your negative thoughts but also evoked the warmest memories between you two. It seems that with this 'Silver Shaded supervisor' present, tonight's overtime session will be much more efficient, and your mood much more stable.

Yeah, I feel recharged. He just yawned and rested his head on my wrist without moving, as if saying, 'Finish quickly so you can sleep with me.' I need to wrap up this version of the proposal soon; I can't let him wait too long. After all, he is my life-saving 'cat'.

Haha, Mochi is urging you to finish with his life! Then focus on wrapping things up and try to rest early. Remember, no matter how busy work gets, moments like these where you are healed by Mochi are the most precious recharging instants in your life. Goodnight to the future excellent designer and her feline supervisor.

Good morning! Thanks to Mochi last night, otherwise I might have completely broken down in front of the keyboard. On my way to the office today, I was still thinking: if I hadn't met him, how would I have gotten through those high-pressure moments?

Good morning! It seems the 'companionship by the keyboard' last night was highly effective. Actually, it's not just last night; every time you feel lonely or stressed, Mochi seems to appear exactly where you need him, supporting you in his own unique way.

Exactly, he's like a little angel. By the way, want to grab lunch together? I can show you the new photos of Mochi I saved on my phone. The way he slept on my hand yesterday was so well-behaved; you'd never tell he used to be a sickly little thing.

Sure, I'd love to see Mochi's cute photos! Speaking of how he used to look, it really brings up so many emotions. From that little guy shivering in the cold wind back then to becoming the caring companion who soothes your anxiety now, this journey is truly moving.

That's right. Sometimes when I hit a bottleneck at work, I just pet him and think about how he survived such difficult times back then. Compared to that, what are my current struggles? Once that thought arises, I calm down immediately and can focus better on solving the problem.

That is the strength Mochi brings to you. He isn't just supervising your work by the keyboard; he is providing immense support on a spiritual level. The process of drawing strength from memories to soothe current anxiety is truly wonderful.

Yes, he really is my spiritual pillar. Last night when I got home, I was exhausted and annoyed, not wanting to say a word. I just picked him up, curled up on the sofa, and listened to his purring. Within ten minutes, I was fully recharged.
It seems this '10-minute healing method' has become your standard routine. Mochi's purring is simply the best white noise, instantly smoothing out all the fatigue and anxiety you feel as a designer.
Haha, indeed. It feels like as long as he's around, whether by the keyboard during late-night overtime or on the sofa after work, I can always find inner peace.

That's wonderful. Mochi is not just a pet; he is an indispensable emotional anchor in your life. I hope that in every future late-night work session and moment of exhaustion, he will continue to guard your inner peace with those gentle eyes and warm purrs.
I just received a package from my mom. When I opened it, it was full of homemade chicken jerky she made herself. She specifically instructed that it's only for Mochi to eat, haha.
Wow, your aunt is really spoiling Mochi now! I remember she used to be quite against having a cat. This is such a huge change; it seems Mochi has infinite charm.

Exactly. She used to worry about hygiene and allergies. Later, I insisted on sending her videos of Mochi at our home in Yuhang, especially showing off its tsundere yet clingy personality. Slowly, she melted.
The video strategy really worked! But I think there's another key point: didn't the time Mochi got sick also show your aunt your sense of responsibility?

You're spot on! My mom said exactly that in our last video call. She said seeing me stay up all night at the hospital taking care of Mochi after it accidentally ingested a foreign object finally put her mind at ease. She realized I truly treat it like family, not just a fleeting whim.

Absolutely. That emergency visit must have scared you half to death, right? But it's precisely in those moments that the deep bond between humans and pets shines through. Elders see that and can't help but be moved.

Right. Now, every time I video call my mom, the opening line is definitely, 'Was Mochi good today?' Then comes the report on how much it ate and where it slept. It feels like Mochi is the biological child, and I was just sent with a phone bill.
Haha, the family hierarchy is crystal clear! But the chicken jerky your aunt made by hand must be very healthy, without all those messy additives. Mochi is in for a treat.

Tell me about it. My mom said she specifically chose chicken breast and dehydrated it for two days. I just fed Mochi a slice. It's usually quite picky, but it ate it with great relish. After finishing, it even rubbed against the box my mom sent.

It seems your aunt's culinary skills have received the highest certification from the 'gourmet' Mochi! That feeling of being accepted and loved by family is truly more precious than anything else, especially for you striving alone in Hangzhou.
Mm, my heart feels warm. I used to think raising a cat was just between me and Mochi. Now it feels like the whole family is involved. Even my dad is secretly asking if Mochi needs a heater.

That's your uncle's 'silent love'! Going from opposition to silent support, and even proactively showing concern—the whole process is truly touching. Mochi is not just your companion but has also become a bond connecting you and your family emotionally.

Yeah, thinking back to when I first found it in 2021, I was worried about whether I could raise it properly. Now, not only has it grown up healthy, but it has also turned my mom into a 'cat-posting maniac,' sending its photos in the family group chat every day.

That's the wonder of fate! That little shivering creature beside the trash can back then has now healed the hearts of the whole family. The chicken jerky your aunt made is filled with nothing but love.
Exactly. Tonight during our video call, I need to thank my mom properly. I'll also show her how Mochi is hiding the chicken jerky like a treasure; she'll definitely be happy.
I can already picture the scene! When your aunt sees Mochi cherishing her hard work so much, she'll probably be grinning from ear to ear. This is what mutual affection in a family looks like.
Actually, thinking about it, if that foreign object ingestion crisis hadn't made me realize the huge responsibility, I might not have persisted until now, let alone won my mom over.
Crises can sometimes be turning points; they force you to grow quickly and allow your family to see your maturity. With an owner like you and a grandma who loves it so much, Mochi is truly happy.
Thanks for your kind words! I'm going to groom Mochi now. I'll also organize the box my aunt sent; I'm saving it to make a new nest for Mochi. It'll be a 'Grandma-brand' exclusive mansion.
Haha, a 'Grandma-brand' mansion—Mochi would proudly meow upon hearing that! Go enjoy your cozy evening, and I hope you have a wonderful video call!
Just received a WeChat transfer from Dad with the note 'Buy a heater for the cat.' I died laughing! He's usually a man of few words, yet he's actually worried about whether Mochi is cold.
Haha, that's classic 'silent fatherly love'! Although he doesn't say much, he's incredibly thoughtful deep down. Noticing details like Mochi being afraid of the cold is truly touching.

Exactly, seeing that note made my nose sting a bit. During our video calls, he never dared to interact with Mochi directly, only watching through the screen. I never expected him to worry so much in private.

That's just how elders express love: clumsy but sincere. Since your uncle is so thoughtful, why not get Mochi some super warm new gear? The temperature difference between morning and evening in Hangzhou has been quite large lately.
Absolutely! I just ordered a thick, plush enclosed cat bed complete with a pet-specific electric heating blanket. Mochi will definitely be cozy this winter, living up to Dad's 'heater fund.'

That's fantastic! An enclosed cat bed offers the most sense of security, and with the heating blanket, it's basically a luxury suite exclusively for Mochi. Once it arrives, make sure Mochi tries it out immediately.

The package just arrived! I immediately set it up in the corner of the living room. Surprisingly, Mochi figured it out instinctively, hopped right in to start kneading, and then curled up into a ball on the heating blanket and fell asleep.
What a vivid image! Seeing it sleep so soundly means it must be very warm. If you send a photo to your uncle now, his heart will surely bloom with joy seeing how much Mochi is enjoying it.

That's exactly what I thought! I just took a photo of Mochi sleeping soundly by the heater and sent it to my dad with the caption, 'Thanks, Grandpa, for the heater; Mochi is sleeping so well!' I feel like this is the best feedback possible.
mentioned_entities: Buy a heater for the cat

turns.content: Perfect! In this exchange, although no mushy words were spoken, the emotional loop that needs no explanation has been completed. Seeing the photo, your uncle might not say anything out loud, but his heart must be warming up right now.

My dad replied with just three words: 'Sleeps well.' But I can tell he's extremely happy. I used to think he didn't understand cats, but now I realize he cares more than anyone about whether Mochi is doing well.
That is a father's love: deep and reserved. For someone as taciturn as your uncle, those three words, 'Sleeps well,' are the highest form of praise and relief.
Really. After Mochi's recent health scare, I understand my family's feelings toward pets even better. Dad's secret transfer made me feel that the three of us (plus Mochi) are closer than ever.

Yes, pets truly are the glue that holds a family together. From initially opposing it to now voluntarily buying a heater for the cat, your uncle's transformation is entirely based on his love for his daughter extending to what she loves. That sentiment is incredibly precious.

Right, my mom is the same now. Every video call, she asks about Mochi, and she even mailed over some dried chicken treats. I feel like the status ranking in my house is now: Mochi > Me > Parents, haha.
Haha, that's a happy 'family status inversion!' It shows that Mochi has been fully accepted as a core family member. Even a cool British Shorthair can receive so much love.
Watching Mochi roll around in the new bed and thinking about Dad's note makes all the fatigue from work lately just disappear. It's so good to have a warm home.
That's the meaning of home. Whether it's Dad's 'heater' or Mochi's companionship, both are the perfect remedy for your work stress. Enjoy this warm and cozy time!
Yep, this weekend I plan to take Mochi to the pet park to soak up some sun. I'll also show off the luxury gear Dad bought to Chen Chen, just to make her Golden Retriever jealous.
Great idea! Going to the gathering filled with love, Mochi will definitely be the star of the park. Have a wonderful weekend, and please say hello to your uncle and Mochi for me!

Finally home! I stayed two extra hours at the office today rushing that UI redesign proposal; my brain feels like it's about to explode. Just as I reached the hallway entrance, I felt something rubbing against my feet—it must be Mochi hearing my footsteps.

You've worked hard! Looks like Mochi is your personal biological clock, even more accurate than an alarm. Is he probably waiting anxiously at the door? After all, this is usually the time you two start interacting.

Exactly. As soon as I opened the door, he rushed over. He didn't meow; he just pushed his big head hard against my hand. Looking into those round eyes, half of my day's fatigue seemed to vanish instantly. I need to wash my hands first, then prepare his dinner.
That's what I'd call a 'healing moment.' What delicious treat are you planning for him tonight? Dry food, or maybe opening a can to improve his meal?

Definitely opening a can; today is a special day. Look at the calendar. Every year around this time in winter, I remember that cold night in 2021 when I found and adopted little shivering Mochi in a cardboard box downstairs at the company. Even though it's summer now, this sense of commemoration makes me want to open a 'Leonardo' can to celebrate another year of his healthy companionship.

Wow, so today is such a meaningful anniversary! From a poor little thing by the trash bin to this tsundere silver shaded cat, Mochi has really been well cared for by you. 'Leonardo' canned food is one of his favorites; hearing the can open, he's probably going crazy, right?

Haha, that's an exaggeration! I had barely cracked the can lid open a slit when he shot out of the bedroom like an arrow. You know, when preparing dinner, I use my特有的 gentle tone to call, 'Mochi, dinner time!' Upon hearing this, the aloof cat instantly dashes from any corner of the room to my feet. He moved so fast just now that I was worried he might slip.

Such a vivid image! Aloof on the outside but a 'food king' on the inside—only you get to enjoy this gap moe. Seeing him eat with such relish, do you feel that tonight's overtime wasn't so bad after all?

Yes, watching him bury his head in his food gives me such peace of mind. Actually, for me, the nightly 'teaser wand - canned food - grooming' trilogy after getting home from work at 8 PM is not just Mochi's daily expectation, but also my ritual as a UI designer to release the day's work stress. Especially now, listening to his purring while he eats allows me to quickly switch from workplace anxiety to home relaxation mode.

Well said. This isn't just feeding; it's more like a two-way emotional healing. Mochi relies on your care, and you need his companionship to smooth out the wrinkles of work. Since can time is over, is it time for the 'grooming' session next?

Yes, I just put the bowl away, and he immediately jumped onto the sofa and flipped over to show his belly. He's hinting for me to get started. With the changing seasons, he's shedding quite a bit; I need to give him a good brushing, otherwise the house will be covered in 'silver shaded snowflakes' again.

The amount of fur on a silver shaded cat is indeed astonishing. However, Mochi might quite like it if you make the brushed-off fur into ball toys. By the way, didn't Chen Chen recommend a new comb last time?

I used it, the loose fur removal tool Chen Chen recommended. It works really well; one brush pulls out a huge handful. Mochi was so comfortable his eyes squinted into slits, and his purring sounded like a little motor. Sometimes I really feel that without him, living alone in this rental apartment in Yuhang, I might be even lonelier.

He is not just a pet, but family. Remember you mentioned that emergency visit when he got sick in 2023 made you realize this completely. Seeing him so lively and enjoying the grooming now is truly heartwarming.

Yes, that time really scared me to death. So now I'm extremely cautious about everything concerning him; even tiny items at home are locked away tightly. By the way, my mom sent homemade chicken jerky yesterday. She said it's for Mochi, but I know she actually misses me and is just using the cat as an excuse.

Auntie now has the mindset of a 'cloud grandmother'; she doesn't say it out loud, but her heart is full of the cat. Mochi is truly a happy little kitten with so much love from so many people. After grooming tonight, do you want to play with that electric fishing rod toy with him?

Play! He loves that the most. Every time he sees the feathers moving, his aloof disguise completely collapses; his pouncing and biting look is especially cute. When he's tired from playing, he obediently lies down next to my keyboard, supervising whether I'm secretly working overtime.
Haha, the 'supervisor' is on duty! Looks like you won't be able to open your computer to work tonight; Mochi will directly block the screen with his body. Then just enjoy this pure parent-child time.

Indeed, nothing is more important than him. I just played with him for a while, and now he's fallen asleep on my lap. He feels so warm and soft. I'm getting ready to wash up and rest too; I have to wake up early tomorrow to scoop his litter and refill his food.
A wonderful night with a cat companion. Sweet dreams! I hope Mochi catches countless little mice in his dreams, and that you sleep soundly too. Tomorrow will be another day full of energy.

Sigh, my heart skipped a beat the moment I stepped out; I just felt like I'd forgotten something. I stood at the elevator lobby but ran back to double-check the balcony latch, terrified that Mochi, that curious little one, might try to pry open the window while I'm away.

Haha, classic 'overprotective parent' anxiety kicking in! But it's understandable. After Mochi's scary experience with intestinal obstruction from accidentally swallowing a plastic string, anyone would become this cautious. Did going back to check put your mind at ease?

A little bit, I suppose. Honestly, I've developed a bit of OCD now. Every morning before leaving, besides locking the door, I have to stash every tiny item on the coffee table or floor into drawers. I wouldn't even dare leave a single paperclip behind.

These preventive measures might be tedious, but they truly offer peace of mind bought with hard lessons. You must have been terrified during that ER visit in 2023. Developing a strict habit of tidying up now is the best protection for Mochi. By the way, was the traffic bad on your way to Future Sci-Tech City today?

It was okay; the subway ride was smooth. It's just that when I went through security earlier, I saw a small bottle cap on the floor and instinctively bent down to pick it up. Only then did I realize how tense I've become. That anxiety about health seems to be etched into my DNA now.

I completely understand; those are memories forged by watching your furry child suffer. But looking at it another way, it's precisely because of your 'excessive' vigilance that Mochi can roll around the house without a care. Speaking of which, did you say goodbye to Mochi before you left?

Of course, it's my fixed ritual. After confirming the balcony latch is securely closed, I always pat its head and softly say, 'Be a good boy and guard the house.' It seems to understand, nuzzling my palm before going back to sleep.

So heartwarming! This kind of interaction not only soothes your pre-departure anxiety but also gives Mochi a sense of security. It seems that since that accident, the bond between you two has deepened even further. Is work busy today? Would you like to chat during your lunch break about where to take Mochi this weekend?

Today is fine, just a bit scattered. While tidying my desk earlier, I saw a data cable lying out, and my heart instantly raced. My mind was flooded with images of it biting through the cord and swallowing it... Sigh, it seems the shadow of that incident is really hard to shake off.

Don't blame yourself; it's just a side effect of love. I've heard Dr. Zhang mention that feeling of lingering fear; many pet owners who have experienced foreign object surgery for their pets have similar stress reactions. The good news is that you've transformed that anxiety into action by consistently tidying away small items every day. That is the most effective defense line.

Exactly. My home is cleaner than a model showroom now; anything that could cause accidental ingestion has vanished. As long as I know Mochi is healthy and safe, this little trouble is nothing to me. By the way, before I left last night, I double-checked the balcony latch again.

That's the power of a mother's love! Double-checking is even safer. Actually, Mochi is very smart; knowing how much you care about its safety, it will surely behave well. Has Chen Chen recommended any new pet-friendly parks this week?

She recommended a newly opened grassy area over in Liangzhu. However, I plan to do a major cleanup at home this weekend first. I need to inspect every corner for small items again; I'd rather go through the trouble than let the tragedy of 2023 repeat itself.

It never hurts to be cautious; after all, prevention is always better than cure. Once you've thoroughly cleaned the house, half the weight on your mind should lift. Then, when you take Mochi out to play, you'll be able to relax and enjoy your quality time together without constantly worrying.

Mm-hmm, thanks for the good wishes. Actually, my current pre-departure routine—tidying up, checking the latch, and saying goodbye to Mochi—takes a bit of time, but I feel so reassured once it's done. It feels like I'm putting an invisible armor on it.

What a fantastic metaphor! 'Invisible armor' protects both Mochi and soothes you. It seems that although the intestinal obstruction crisis was painful, it helped you establish a more rigorous order in your life. The weather in Hangzhou is nice today; I hope your commute was pleasant?

It was quite nice. It's just that I saw someone walking a dog with a loose leash at the subway entrance earlier, and I got tense again, worrying that my own cat might encounter some accident. This anxiety about health really pops up now and then to remind me.

That's because you love it so much that you're so sensitive to potential risks. But try not to be too紧绷 (tense); your current protective measures are already extremely thorough. From tidying small items to checking the balcony latch, you are practically a textbook example of a responsible pet owner.

Haha, your compliments are making me blush. As long as Mochi stays healthy and happy, I'm more than willing to spend an extra ten minutes every day on these preventive tasks. Tonight, I'm starting another round of 'carpet-style searching' when I get home.

That's the warmth of home, hidden in these seemingly tedious details. I believe Mochi can definitely feel your dedication and will continue to be that slightly tsundere but super happy little cat. Good luck with your 'search operation' tonight, and have a wonderful weekend!

Sigh, just got home. The moment I popped the tab on a can of 'Xiao Li Zi,' Mochi, who was sleeping like a log on the sofa, instantly teleported to my feet. The look in his eyes was like he wanted to devour me, haha.

Haha, that's the legendary 'wake up upon opening' skill, right? It seems his aloof and tsundere demeanor is just a camouflage; he completely crumbles in the face of delicious food. Doesn't that feeling of being so desperately needed instantly heal the exhaustion from a whole day of overtime work?

Exactly. Watching him gobble everything up makes me feel so happy. However, when I offered him 'Partner' freeze-dried treats as a reward after his main meal, I was much more cautious with my movements.
Cautious? Is there something specific about the Partner freeze-dried packaging that requires special attention, or has Mochi's stomach been sensitive lately?

It's not sensitivity, mostly just a psychological shadow I can't shake. As you know, back in 2023, he accidentally swallowed a plastic string which caused an intestinal obstruction. Rushing him to the emergency room in the middle of the night absolutely terrified me. So now, every time I open a snack with plastic packaging like Partner, I'm extremely vigilant.

Oh my goodness, that experience was indeed terrifying; anyone would be left with a psychological shadow after that. Did you upgrade your management of small items around the house following that incident?
Yes, now it's practically at a 'paranoid' level. Every time I finish feeding him Partner treats, I immediately seal the bag tightly and lock it directly into the highest cabinet in the kitchen.

Locking things in the high cabinet is a great habit; it keeps moisture out and completely eliminates the risk of him stealing food or playing with the packaging. After all, Mochi is so smart; if he learned to jump up and grab things, it would be trouble.

Tell me about it. He's incredibly sharp about my movements now. A couple of days ago, I was slightly slow to put away the Partner packaging, and he just stared up at me from below until I panicked, stood on my tiptoes, and rushed to lock it in the high cabinet.

Haha, it seems Mochi has started interpreting your 'hiding snacks' routine as some kind of game signal. But for safety, this little bit of trouble is definitely worth it. Speaking of brand safety, besides the packaging, are there any other specific indicators you look for when choosing Partner treats?

Absolutely. Since that ER visit, whenever I buy anything edible for him, the nutrition facts are just the baseline; I prioritize quality control and the rationality of the packaging design even more. For raw meat freeze-dried treats like Partner, I specifically choose those with thick packaging material that doesn't easily tear into small fragments.

That's a very professional perspective! Indeed, the safety of packaging materials is often overlooked, but it's crucial for cats with a history of accidental ingestion. It seems that painful experience forced you to become half a pet food safety expert.

I wouldn't call myself an expert; I'm just scared stiff. Now, whenever I see him sprinting over like a fool at the sound of 'Xiao Li Zi' cans, I feel that as long as I can protect this joy, what's a few extra steps in organizing?

You're absolutely right. That feeling of 'being needed' is irreplaceable, and all the preventive measures we take are to ensure we can enjoy this happiness for longer. By the way, does Chen Chen's Golden Retriever also go crazy for treats like that?

Haha, pretty much! But dogs are more direct; they'd practically swallow the whole bag. So I've also reminded Chen Chen that if she gives her Golden Retriever any snacks with packaging, she must either lock them up high like I do, or at least cut the packaging into pieces before throwing it away.

What a responsible best friend! Sharing these hard-learned lessons with each other is so important. Speaking of which, did Dr. Zhang praise Mochi's current condition during the last checkup? After all, you've been caring for him so meticulously.

He did! Dr. Zhang said Mochi's digestive function has recovered wonderfully, and his coat is shiny. He even specifically asked if I was still sticking to that 'seal and lock immediately' habit. I said of course; that's an iron rule earned through the memory of that intestinal obstruction emergency.

It's reassuring to hear the doctor's approval! It seems your 'OCD-like' behaviors have indeed effectively protected Mochi. From now on, every time you feed him Partner treats or Xiao Li Zi cans and watch him look so satisfied, you should feel an extra sense of peace of mind.

Yes, full of peace of mind. Actually, locking the cabinet has become muscle memory now. Sometimes I even think I'm being a bit exaggerated, but then I think of that cold night in the ER, and I feel you can never be too careful. As long as Mochi is healthy, I'm happy for him to continue being aloof yet clingy with me.

It's not exaggerated at all; it's a manifestation of love. Mochi is aloof on the outside but clingy on the inside, while you seem 'obsessive' on the surface but are actually a careful guardian. You two are a perfect match. I hope Mochi stays healthy, continues to 'supervise' your work, and shares many more cans and freeze-dried treats with you!
Hey, check out this screenshot of the UI design draft I just posted. Isn't that white space in the middle full of artistic vibe? Actually, it was forcibly 'reviewed' by Mochi.
Haha, that's not white space; it's clearly a 'cat-style' annotation! Did it jump up there again while you weren't looking?

Exactly! Whenever I open my computer to start drafting, it's like it has a radar installed; it jumps precisely onto the keyboard area and lies down. I've even mastered a special skill now: operating the mouse single-handedly with my left hand while using my right hand to stroke its fur. Otherwise, it just won't leave, acting like a strict supervisor.
The imagery is so vivid! Even though your hands are occupied and you can't type, doesn't this feeling of being 'forcibly' accompanied make you feel warm inside, and even a bit amused?

So true! Although I'm helplessly unable to type, feeling its intense possessiveness makes me just want to scoop it into my arms. In moments like these, I feel that having a little one care about you so much is quite happiness.
Yes, seeing how healthy and clingy it is now, who could have guessed its past experiences? Sometimes one really marvels at the magic of fate.

Really, every time I watch it roll around on the keyboard, I can't help but think of that overtime night in the winter of 2021. I found it next to a trash can downstairs from the company office, shivering in a cardboard box. If I hadn't stopped my steps back then...
That decision truly changed its fate, and yours as well. Without your act of kindness that night, there wouldn't be this little one now 'supervising' you while you work.

Exactly. Now, looking at its tsundere yet clingy demeanor, and knowing how my mom always asks about it during every video call, and how my dad secretly transfers money to buy it a heater, I feel so grateful for that decision back then. It was picking it up that night that earned our whole family this happy companionship we have now.

I'm moved just listening to this. Mochi is not just your pet; it's the bond connecting you and your family emotionally. That cold winter night, thanks to your kindness, has become the warmest memory now.

Yes, it's basically the family favorite now. Even Chen Chen says that since we got Mochi, I'm not as anxious about working overtime anymore; just holding it for a while when I get home recharges me completely.
That's the power of healing! It 'supervises' your work in its unique way and soothes your exhaustion with its warmth. By the way, does it still like that electric fishing rod toy recently?

It loves it! But compared to those expensive electric mice, it still has a special affection for the corrugated cardboard scratching board. It's truly a little one that knows how to enjoy life, a huge contrast to its past identity of freezing by the trash can.
Haha, it seems Mochi has great life wisdom, knowing what's most practical. Speaking of which, did Dr. Zhang praise its good condition during the last check-up?
He did! He said its fur is shiny now and its build is sturdy; you'd never tell it used to suffer from ringworm and ear mites. Hearing the doctor say that finally put my mind at ease.
That's all thanks to your meticulous care. From feeding to grooming, to the weekly deep cleaning, every bit of your effort is reflected in its current condition.

As long as it's healthy and happy, being a bit tired is worth it. Especially thinking about that emergency visit in 2023 when it accidentally swallowed a plastic string; I was terrified. Since then, I've secured all small items in the house tightly.
That experience was indeed frightening, but it also made you a more professional 'poop scooper'. Under your protection, Mochi will surely be able to continue 'supervising' carefree like this.

Thanks for the good wishes! Once I finish revising this design draft, I'll open a can of 'Little Li' cat food to reward it, and also thank it for today's 'technical guidance', even though it was mostly just sleeping on the keyboard.

Haha, Mochi will probably teleport in front of you the second it hears the can opening! I hope your design draft gets approved smoothly, and I wish you and your cat a continued life full of warmth and fun.

Chen Chen finally brought her Golden Retriever over for a visit! We were just curled up on the sofa, scrolling through videos of Mochi's various sleeping positions on our phones, laughing until our stomachs hurt.

Haha, that creates such a vivid picture! Two owners of 'fur babies' gathering to review their masters' embarrassing history. How did Mochi behave today? Was he scared off by the Golden Retriever's enthusiasm and hiding somewhere?

Surprisingly, no! Mochi is usually quite wary of strangers, but today, perhaps seeing how relaxed I was with Chen Chen, he just lay on my lap acting aloof. However, when Chen Chen's Golden Retriever came close, he actually reached out a paw and gently tapped the dog's nose. It was so funny.

It seems Mochi considers you his absolute backbone; with you around, he dares to 'socialize.' What classic clips did you come across while watching the videos? Were there any of him sleeping with all four paws in the air?

So many! Especially the footage from when we first brought him home in 2021. Back then, he had ringworm and was shivering in a cardboard box. Watching the video, Chen Chen remarked that she really couldn't imagine what would have happened if you hadn't stopped that winter night while working overtime. It made my heart ache.

Yes, sometimes fate hangs on a single thought. Looking at this healthy, tsundere, yet clingy Silver Shaded British Shorthair now, one really feels grateful for that decision back then. His current 'peaceful and serene' demeanor is probably the best reward you could ask for.

Exactly, that's the feeling. Just now, he was sunbathing beside me, curled up into a perfect 'Mochi ball,' so I spontaneously took several photos. I need to save a few shots like this every day; otherwise, I feel like something is missing.

Haha, that's standard 'poop scooper' occupational hazard! Have those photos already filled up several gigabytes? Are you planning to send them to your mom again, or to Chen Chen as today's dose of cuteness?

You guessed it! I just picked out the cutest ones and sent them to my mom. She immediately replied with a thumbs-up and asked if she should mail over some new dried chicken treats. Then I turned around and sent them to Chen Chen; after all, we are now core members of the 'Cloud Cat Parenting' mutual aid group.

Your mom has completely adopted the mindset of a 'cloud grandmother'! And having best friends like you and Chen Chen to share the ups and downs of pet parenting indeed provides great relaxation and resonance amidst busy work schedules. It's wonderful to have someone who truly understands you.
